title: In the Beginning
subtitle: God creates the heavens and the earth

pages.title: LET THERE BE LIGHT
pages.subtitle: Before time. Before space. Before everything — there was God. And then He spoke.

pages.verse: In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.

originalWord.meaning: in the beginning, at the first

pages.note: Ten words in Hebrew. The most important sentence ever written. Before this verse, there was nothing but God. After it, there was everything. The Hebrew word 'bara' (created) is used only of God — it means to create from absolute nothing.

panels.narration: And the earth was without form, and void; and darkness was upon the face of the deep. And the Spirit of God moved upon the face of the waters.

pages.title: AND THERE WAS LIGHT
pages.text: And God said, Let there be light: and there was light. And God saw the light, that it was good: and God divided the light from the darkness.

panels.narration: And God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night. And the evening and the morning were the first day.

And God said, Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters, and let it divide the waters from the waters. And God called the firmament Heaven. And the evening and the morning were the second day.

And God said, Let the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place, and let the dry land appear: and it was so. And the earth brought forth grass, and herb yielding seed, and the fruit tree yielding fruit. And the evening and the morning were the third day.

kids.question: God made light just by speaking! What is your favourite thing that God made?
kids.activity: Draw the first three days of creation: light, sky, and land with plants!

teens.question: God created order from chaos — light from darkness, land from water, life from nothing. Where in your life do you need God to bring order out of chaos?
teens.journal: Write about a time something beautiful came out of a messy situation.

adults.question: The repeated pattern 'And God said… and it was so' reveals creation by divine speech. The Word of God is not merely informational — it is performative. It does what it says. How does this shape your understanding of Scripture's authority?
adults.application: Consider the areas of 'formless void' in your life. What would it look like to invite God's creative Word into those spaces?

episodes.title: The Heavens Declare
episodes.subtitle: Sun, moon, stars, and every living creature — Days 4 to 6

pages.title: THE HEAVENS DECLARE
pages.subtitle: Stars flung across infinity. Creatures that swim, fly, and roam. The canvas fills with wonder.

panels.narration: And God made two great lights; the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night: he made the stars also. And God set them in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth.

And God said, Let the waters bring forth abundantly the moving creature that hath life, and fowl that may fly above the earth in the open firmament of heaven. And God created great whales, and every living creature that moveth.

speech.speaker: God
speech.text: Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let fowl multiply in the earth.

panels.narration: And God made the beast of the earth after his kind, and cattle after their kind, and every thing that creepeth upon the earth after his kind: and God saw that it was good.
Four words that shake the universe: 'He made the stars also.' Billions of galaxies, each containing billions of stars — dismissed in a half-sentence. To God, the stars are an afterthought.

pages.verse: And God made two great lights; the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night: he made the stars also.
pages.note: 'He made the stars also' — possibly the most understated sentence in all literature. Astronomers estimate over 200 billion trillion stars exist. Genesis records their creation in four words as a footnote. The God of the Bible is that big.

kids.question: God made all the animals! If you could create a new animal, what would it look like?
kids.activity: Draw your three favourite animals and thank God for making them!

teens.question: 'He made the stars also' — billions of galaxies summarised in four words. What does that tell you about the scale of God versus the scale of your problems?
teens.journal: Go outside tonight and look at the stars. Write about how it makes you feel knowing the God who made them knows your name.

adults.question: The creation narrative moves from the cosmic (light, sky, seas) to the particular (fish, birds, land animals). God is both transcendently vast and intimately detailed. How do you hold both truths — God's cosmic sovereignty and His personal care — in tension?
adults.application: This week, practise 'double vision': when you see something vast (sky, ocean, mountains), praise God's greatness. When you see something small (a sparrow, a flower), praise His attention to detail.

episodes.title: In His Image
episodes.subtitle: The crown of creation — God makes humanity

pages.title: IN HIS IMAGE
pages.subtitle: Everything else, God spoke into being. But for humanity, God got His hands dirty.

speech.speaker: God
speech.text: Let us make man in our image, after our likeness: and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over the cattle, and over all the earth.

panels.narration: So God created man in his own image, in the image of God created he him; male and female created he them.

pages.title: VERY GOOD
pages.text: And God saw every thing that he had made, and, behold, it was very good. And the evening and the morning were the sixth day.

panels.narration: And the LORD God formed man of the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living soul.
On the seventh day God ended his work which he had made; and he rested on the seventh day from all his work. And God blessed the seventh day, and sanctified it.

pages.verse: And the LORD God formed man of the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living soul.

originalWord.meaning: breath, spirit (the divine breath of life)

pages.note: God spoke everything else into existence. But He formed man with His hands (yatsar — like a potter) and breathed His own breath into him. Humanity is not just created — we carry the breath of God Himself. This is the source of human dignity.

kids.question: God made YOU in His image! That means you are very special. What do you think it means to be made in God's image?
kids.activity: Draw a self-portrait and write 'Made in God's Image' at the top!

teens.question: Every human being — regardless of race, ability, status, or appearance — is made in the image of God. How should that change the way you treat people you disagree with, or people the world overlooks?
teens.journal: Write about someone you find hard to respect. Now consider: they carry the image of God. What changes?

adults.question: After each previous day, God said 'it was good.' After creating humanity, He said 'it was very good.' The Hebrew 'tov me'od' implies completion, wholeness. Humanity was the capstone that made creation complete. What does the imago Dei mean for human rights, dignity, and the sanctity of life?
adults.application: This week, consciously practise seeing every person you encounter — the barista, the difficult colleague, the stranger — as an image-bearer of God. How does it change your interactions?

episodes.title: The Garden
episodes.subtitle: Paradise planted, a helper fashioned, and a single command

pages.title: THE GARDEN
pages.subtitle: A paradise of beauty. A river with four branches. Every tree pleasant to the sight. And one tree forbidden.

panels.narration: And the LORD God planted a garden eastward in Eden; and there he put the man whom he had formed. And out of the ground made the LORD God to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight, and good for food; the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of knowledge of good and evil.

speech.speaker: God
speech.text: Of every tree of the garden thou mayest freely eat: But of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, thou shalt not eat of it: for in the day that thou eatest thereof thou shalt surely die.

panels.narration: And the LORD God said, It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him an help meet for him.

And out of the ground the LORD God formed every beast of the field, and every fowl of the air; and brought them unto Adam to see what he would call them: and whatsoever Adam called every living creature, that was the name thereof.

And the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon Adam, and he slept: and he took one of his ribs, and closed up the flesh. And the rib, which the LORD God had taken from man, made he a woman, and brought her unto the man.

pages.title: BONE OF MY BONES
pages.text: And Adam said, This is now bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh: she shall be called Woman, because she was taken out of Man.

pages.verse: And the LORD God said, It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him an help meet for him.

originalWord.meaning: a helper corresponding to him, a counterpart

pages.note: The word 'ezer' (helper) is used 21 times in the Old Testament — 16 of those times it refers to God Himself as Israel's helper. This is not a word of subordination but of strength. Eve was not made inferior; she was made indispensable.

kids.question: Adam got to name all the animals! If you could name a new animal, what would you call it?
kids.activity: Draw the Garden of Eden with your favourite animals, trees, and a big river!

teens.question: God gave Adam incredible freedom — 'of every tree thou mayest freely eat' — with only one restriction. Why do you think we always focus on the one thing we can't have instead of the thousands of things we can?
teens.journal: Make a list of 10 freedoms and blessings you have. Then write about the one restriction that bugs you most. Which list is longer?

adults.question: The first 'not good' in Scripture isn't sin — it's isolation. 'It is not good that the man should be alone.' God designed humans for relationship before the fall. How does this shape our theology of community, marriage, and the church?
adults.application: Evaluate your relational life honestly. Are you trying to live the Christian life alone? Where is God saying 'it is not good' to your isolation?
